---
title: "RLE Assessment Workshop"
subtitle: "Tools for Red List of Ecosystems Assessments"
author:
  - name: "Tyler Erickson"
  - name: "Tanya Birch"
date: "2026-04-15"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    slide-number: true
    navigation-mode: vertical
    footer: "RLE Assessment Workshop"
---

## Welcome

This workshop introduces a set of tools to facilitate conducting **Red List of Ecosystems (RLE)** assessments.

::: {.incremental}
- What is the RLE?
- Available tools and data
- Hands-on examples
:::

<!-- ## What is the RLE?

The IUCN Red List of Ecosystems is a global standard for assessing the conservation status of ecosystems.

. . .

It evaluates ecosystems against five criteria:

::: {.incremental}
1. Decline in distribution
2. Restricted distribution
3. Environmental degradation
4. Disruption of biotic processes
5. Quantitative risk analysis
::: -->

<!-- ## Assessment Categories

| Category | Abbreviation |
|----------|:------------:|
| Collapsed | CO |
| Critically Endangered | CR |
| Endangered | EN |
| Vulnerable | VU |
| Near Threatened | NT |
| Least Concern | LC | -->

# Getting Started

## Prerequisites

1. A [Google Cloud Project (GCP)](https://console.cloud.google.com) account
2. A [GitHub](https://github.com/) account
3. A development environment with tools installed:
    - [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) package/environment manager
    - [Google Cloud CLI (`gcloud`)](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install) command-line tool
    - [GitHub CLI (`gh`)](https://cli.github.com) command-line tool

## Development Environment Options

| Option | Pros | Cons |
|--------|--------|--------|
| Google Cloud Shell | Uniform development environment for all users | Limited storage |
| Local computer | A local development environment | Requires personalized setup |

# Tools

***TODO: Show overview diagram of tools and data flow.***

## Tools Overview

- `iucn-get-data` — [Global Ecosystem Typology (GET) data](https://global-ecosystems.org/)
- `rle-python-gee` — Python tools for GEE-based assessments
    - Based on [IUCN RLE Publications](https://iucnrle.org/documents_and_publications)

## iucn-get-data


## Example: Topology class {.smaller}

```{python}
#| echo: true
from iucn_get_data import Typology
from pprint import pprint

typology = Typology(language='spanish')
print(typology)
```

## Example: Display biomes in a realm {.smaller}

```{python}
#| echo: true
[f'{b.code}: {b.name}' for b in typology.realms['T'].biomes.values()]
```


# Technical walkthrough

# Demo Example

# Colombia Example

# Next Steps

::: {.callout-tip}
## Resources
- [IUCN RLE Website](https://iucnrle.org/)
- [RLE Assessment GitHub](https://github.com/RLE-Assessment)
:::

. . .

**Thank you!**

Questions?
